name: Calcena - Purujosa - Trasobares - Calcena — circular por la Cara Oculta del Moncayo
region_intro: Mountain biking around Calcena offers diverse landscapes within the southern foothills of the Moncayo Natural Park in Zaragoza, Spain. The region features varied terrain, including kermes oak forests, abandoned vineyards, and areas with distinctive limestone formations. Riders can expect significant elevation changes and technical sections, making it a destination for challenging mountain bike trails. The area is characterized by dry ravines, riverside vegetation, and panoramic viewpoints. Best mountain bike trails around Calcena The most popular mountain bike trail is Calcena - Purujosa - Trasobares - Calcena — circular por la Cara Oculta del Moncayo, a difficult 34.0 miles (54.8 km) trail that takes about 5 hours 51 minutes to complete. This route explores the "Cara Oculta del Moncayo" with substantial elevation gain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cerro Gordo MTB Route, a moderate 18.0 miles (29.0 km) path. This route features varied terrain and offers views of the surrounding natural environment. Local mountain bikers also love the Vistas a La Muela de Horcajuelo, Cerro Morrón y las Peñas de Herrera — circular desde Añón de Moncayo, a 26.5 miles (42.6 km) trail leading through elevated landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 23 minutes. Mountain biking around Calcena is defined by challenging ascents, technical descents through ravines, and scenic paths through kermes oak forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Calcena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 50 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Calcena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Calcena?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails around Calcena, offering a range of experiences from moderate to difficult. The area is known for its challenging ascents and technical descents.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain biking in Calcena?
A: Mountain biking in Calcena generally ranges from moderate to difficult. Many routes feature significant elevation changes and technical sections, including rocky terrain in ravines, requiring good fitness and advanced riding skills. Some parts may even necessitate pushing your bike.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly mountain bike trails in Calcena?
A: While many trails are challenging, there are some moderate options available. The guide data indicates 9 moderate trails out of 20. For truly easy or family-friendly routes, it's best to check individual trail descriptions on komoot for specific details on terrain and elevation suitable for all skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails around Calcena?
A: The terrain around Calcena is diverse, featuring riverside vegetation, abandoned vineyards, dense kermes oak forests, and areas with distinctive limestone formations. You'll encounter dirt tracks, technical single tracks, and rocky sections in ravines, especially on descents.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Calcena?
A: The region offers stunning natural beauty. You can expect panoramic views from points like the Chapel of San Cristóbal de Calcena. Other notable features include the &lt;em&gt;Barranco de Valcongosto&lt;/em&gt;, a dry ravine with a small stream, and unique rock formations. You might also encounter natural arches like &lt;em&gt;Puente de los Lobos&lt;/em&gt;. For specific viewpoints, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de la Mata&lt;/a&gt; or enjoying the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5304172" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Moncayo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Calcena?
A: Yes, many routes in Calcena are circular. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986809904/calcena-purujosa-trasobares-calcena-circular-por-la-cara-oculta-del-moncayo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calcena - Purujosa - Trasobares - Calcena — circular por la Cara Oculta del Moncayo&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the 'Cara Oculta del Moncayo'. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986762639/vistas-a-la-muela-de-horcajuelo-cerro-morron-y-las-penas-de-herrera-circular-desde-anon-de-moncayo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vistas a La Muela de Horcajuelo, Cerro Morrón y las Peñas de Herrera — circular desde Añón de Moncayo&lt;/a&gt;, which offers elevated landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Calcena?
A: Calcena is situated in the southern foothills of the Moncayo Natural Park, which can experience varied weather. Generally, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking, with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, and some areas might be dry, while winters can bring colder temperatures and potentially snow at higher elevations.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Calcena?
A: The mountain bike trails around Calcena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, challenging ascents, and technical descents through ravines, as well as the scenic paths through kermes oak forests and the panoramic views.

Q: Are there any specific attractions or points of interest near the mountain biking routes?
A: Beyond the trails, you can explore historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Pilares&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the nearby settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Borobia&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5943916" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soriano Water Nature Trail&lt;/a&gt; also offers a different natural experience.

Q: What are the typical durations for mountain bike routes in Calcena?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance, elevation, and difficulty. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986742842/cerro-gordo-mtb-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Gordo MTB Route&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 29.0 km, typically takes around 2 hours 50 minutes. Longer, more difficult routes like the 42.6 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986762639/vistas-a-la-muela-de-horcajuelo, cerro-morron-y-las-penas-de-herrera-circular-desde-anon-de-moncayo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vistas a La Muela de Horcajuelo, Cerro Morrón y las Peñas de Herrera — circular desde Añón de Moncayo&lt;/a&gt; can take over 4 hours.

Q: Are there options for accommodation or places to eat in Calcena?
A: Calcena is a small village, but as an emerging destination for outdoor enthusiasts, it offers some basic amenities. While specific establishments are not detailed in the guide data, you can typically find local accommodation and dining options in the village itself or in nearby towns within the Moncayo Natural Park area.

Q: Is it possible to find less crowded mountain bike trails in Calcena?
A: Calcena is considered a less-explored corner of Aragon, making it a good option for those seeking quieter trails compared to more popular mountain biking destinations. While some routes, like those part of the 'Cara Oculta del Moncayo', are well-known locally, the overall region offers a sense of adventure away from large crowds.
tips:
The Mirador de la Ermita de San Cristóbal is located in the municipality of Los Fayos, in the province of Zaragoza, Aragon. This viewpoint offers spectacular views of the Queiles River Valley and the surrounding area, including the Val reservoir.

The San Cristóbal hermitage, located next to the viewpoint, is a place of great historical and cultural interest. Built in the 17th century, the hermitage is an example of religious architecture in the region. Access to the viewpoint and the hermitage can be made through several hiking routes that run through the area, offering the opportunity to enjoy nature and panoramic views.

The Cerrogordo refuge, in Purujosa, is located at an altitude of 1,298 meters, in a clear area that allows you to contemplate the panoramic views of the surrounding mountains. This shelter is not guarded, but it does have a fireplace and stone benches to sit on. In addition, the residents of Purujosa have installed a sideboard inside where they can place the food that visitors leave as a provision for others who may need it.
From this point we will have beautiful views of the Cueva de los Pilares, a spectacular spot in the Moncayo Natural Park, at the foot of the Col millstone.
